title: The Too-Perfect New Neighbor
omen: a dog barking at an empty gazebo
relationship_dynamic: protagonist internet-searches her own murder in advance

starter_premise.title: The Too-Perfect New Neighbor
starter_premise.premise: His wife died under mysterious circumstances and he speaks of her with just a little too much fondness.
starter_premise.signature_tell: The framed photo of the dead wife is always slightly off-center.
starter_premise.base_premise: He's charming, bakes, fixes things, and has zero history plus zero last name.

props: locked room key on an optimistic keychain

traits: can identify gaslighting by sentence three
still apologizes before telling the truth
keeps screenshots like sacred texts
secret_capacity: recognizes a love-bomb by floral arrangement density

cast.suspicion_profile: appears trustworthy under pendant lighting only

cast.name: Elias Winterson

wins over children by the second breakfast

already knows where the family documents are kept
cast.regional_responsibility: is trusted to lock up after emotionally charged events

cast.name: Daniel Cross
traits: distrusts every man immediately
emotionally precise
notices timeline errors before adults do

cast.name: Mara Bennett
traits: knows the old case too well
definitely has rain-scene potential
hovers between competence and grief

contradiction_ledger.entry: Wyatt Pruitt is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Noah Pike says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Blackthorn Lake.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Wyatt?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Daniel. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Noah. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The blood-adjacent tea towel is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Daniel."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
"Honey, ever since the accident, your anxiety has been so high. Did you take your pills?"
"That pool was supposed to make us look safe, and somehow it became a witness."
"He keeps calling it stress like stress knows my garage code."
"If she is the nanny, why is she wearing my robe like a legal argument?"
fake_critic_blurb: "The Too-Perfect New Neighbor understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "The Too-Perfect New Neighbor" follows Noah Pike, a recovery protagonist with paperwork caught inside protagonist internet-searches her own murder in advance before she understood where the gap had already opened. Wyatt Pruitt collides with her life carrying portable suspicion, plausible access to offsite storage, and the kind of calm that sounds premeditated in a dim kitchen. The governing injury is simple: His wife died under mysterious circumstances and he speaks of her with just a little too much fondness. The signature tell is always this: The framed photo of the dead wife is always slightly off-center. As a dog barking at an empty gazebo, what she cannot yet see becomes the syntax of the whole county, and every withheld fact keeps breathing just ahead of her. Where every secret comes home.
